is the intention to put up plant
that will surpass any in the west.
The latest hydraulic machinery will
be used and the dry process which
has made st. Louis brick famous,
will be used in the operation of the
business.


A clay bed of large proportions is
located on the land which will be
utilized for the brick manufacture.


representative of the company
will go east for the machinery with
in few days, the massiveness of
which can be realized when it is
shown that it requires two months
to get it into place.


The water power will supply the
works with electricity and an album
dance of power will be left which
will be for sale. The shipping facil-
tiles cannot be surpassed.


The company will incorporate with
a capilization of $4,000 and promi-
nent business men of the city have
stockholders.


The eastern syndicate has secured
an option on the land adjoining that
Of the Spokane company.


Many estimates have been made
upon the possibility of the building
of town at that point. Trent is
about mile from the proposed site
and 1t will, no doubt, receive the
benefit of the new factory site and
location.
